title: ophrys apifera var. saraepontana
intro: Ophrys apifera var. saraepontana, also known as the bee orchid, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Ophrys apifera plant. It is a type of orchid native to Europe, characterized by its unique flowers that mimic the appearance of bees, which attracts pollinators. Traditionally, the bee orchid has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the bee orchid is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
